title: SQL Foundations
subtitle: Learn to read tables, write SELECT queries, filter rows, sort results, and handle real-world beginner data.
intendedFor: web developers who need SQL basics
learningGoals: Explain what SQL does in plain language.
Identify tables, rows, columns, records, fields, and simple keys.
Write basic SELECT queries against one table.
Choose specific columns instead of always using SELECT *.
Filter rows with WHERE.
Compare text and numeric values.
Combine conditions with AND, OR, and NOT.
Sort results with ORDER BY.
Limit output with LIMIT.
Search text using LIKE and wildcards.
Filter lists and ranges using IN, NOT IN, and BETWEEN.
Find missing values using IS NULL and IS NOT NULL.
Read query results and explain them in plain English.

modules.title: Getting Started with SQL and Databases
modules.purpose: Build the learner's mental model before syntax: what SQL is, how databases organize information, and how questions become queries.
learningObjectives: Explain what SQL is used for in real applications.
Identify tables, rows, columns, records, and fields.
Read simple table data and describe what it means.
Use the browser SQL workspace to run a beginner query.

topics.title: What SQL Means
topics.summary: Understand what SQL means so you can explain the idea in beginner-friendly language.
learningGoals: Describe what SQL means in plain language.
Identify a beginner SQL scenario that depends on understanding what SQL means.

modules.title: Your First Queries with SELECT
modules.purpose: Teach learners to retrieve data confidently with SELECT and FROM before filtering or joining tables.
learningObjectives: Write SELECT queries that retrieve data from one table.
Choose specific columns instead of always selecting every column.
Use FROM to identify the table being queried.
Read a query result table and explain what was returned.

modules.title: Filtering Data with WHERE
modules.purpose: Introduce decision-making in queries by teaching learners to keep only the rows they actually need.
learningObjectives: Use WHERE to return only rows that match a condition.
Compare text and numbers with beginner SQL operators.
Combine conditions with AND, OR, and NOT.
Debug common filtering mistakes in simple SQL queries.

Use LIMIT to show a smaller result set.

Find missing and present values with IS NULL and IS NOT NULL.
